descriptions: The Robert's Lounge Crew was associated with the Lucchese family through Burke's friendship with Vario.
edges.evidence_quote: Although Burke's crew was independent, many members were associates of the Lucchese family through Burke's and Vario's longtime friendship.

descriptions: The Robert's Lounge Crew cooperated with the Vario Crew through the friendship between Burke and Vario.
edges.evidence_quote: Burke led his semi-independent Robert's Lounge Crew , from his bar Robert's Lounge . The Robert's Lounge crew was composed of numerous people who were involved in armed robbery, hijacking, and murder. Although Burke's crew was independent, many members were associates of the Lucchese family through Burke's and Vario's longtime friendship.

descriptions: BWF formed from younger TMD members.
edges.evidence_quote: Some of the younger members of TMD had begun to associate with the London Bloods movement and began calling themselves Ida Boys (also known as Ida Rydaz, Ida Bloodstarz, or IDA), Bloodline or Bloodgang, and BWF (Broadwater Farm).

descriptions: Ghost Shadows had links to Sicilian-American Mafia families.
edges.evidence_quote: The Ghost Shadows' influence was widespread, having links to Chinatowns in other cities, as well as links to Sicilian-American Mafia families.

descriptions: Lo Bianco and Barba are allies within the Locale di Vibo Valentia.
edges.evidence_quote: I Lo Bianco sono una 'ndrina calabrese di Vibo Valentia alleata dei Barba all'interno dell'organigramma del Locale di Vibo Valentia.

Murder, Inc..description: Murder, Inc. was an organized crime group active from 1929 to 1941 that served as the enforcement arm of the National Crime Syndicate. Composed of Jewish-American and Italian-American gangsters, it was based in New York City and responsible for hundreds of contract killings. The group was exposed in 1941 by former member Abe Reles, leading to convictions and executions of many members.

descriptions: The Commission ordered Murder, Inc. leader Lepke to kill Dutch Schultz.
edges.evidence_quote: the Commission ordered Lepke Buchalter to eliminate Schultz

descriptions: Bugs and Meyer Mob was a predecessor to Murder, Inc.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bugs and Meyer mob acted as a predecessor to Murder, Inc.

descriptions: The Bugs and Meyer Mob was absorbed into Murder, Inc.
edges.evidence_quote: the Jewish Bugs and Meyer Mob operated on the Lower East Side of Manhattan before being absorbed into Murder, Inc.

descriptions: The Bugs and Meyer Mob was the predecessor to Murder, Inc.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bugs and Meyer Mob was the predecessor to Murder, Incorporated.

descriptions: The Mangano family (predecessor of Gambino) used Murder, Inc. as a cover for contract killings.
edges.evidence_quote: Vincent Mangano also established the City Democratic Club, ostensibly to promote American values. In reality, the club was a cover for Murder, Inc., the notorious band of mainly Jewish hitmen who performed contract murders for the Cosa Nostra nationwide.

descriptions: Albert Anastasia, underboss of the Gambino family, led Murder, Inc.
edges.evidence_quote: Its most prominent member during this time was its underboss Albert Anastasia, who rose to infamy as the operating head of the underworld's enforcement arm, Murder, Inc.

descriptions: The Gambino family's underboss led Murder, Inc., indicating a structural tie.
edges.evidence_quote: Its most prominent member during this time was its underboss Albert Anastasia, who rose to infamy as the operating head of the underworld's enforcement arm, Murder, Inc.

descriptions: Albert Anastasia, underboss of the Gambino family, led Murder, Inc., indicating a structural tie.
edges.evidence_quote: Its most prominent member during this time was its underboss Albert Anastasia, who rose to infamy as the operating head of the underworld's enforcement arm, Murder, Inc.

descriptions: Albert Anastasia, underboss of the Gambino family, headed Murder, Inc.
edges.evidence_quote: Its most prominent member during this time was its underboss Albert Anastasia, who rose to infamy as the operating head of the underworld's enforcement arm, Murder, Inc.

descriptions: Albert Anastasia, underboss of the Gambino family, was the head of Murder, Inc.
edges.evidence_quote: Its most prominent member during this time was its underboss Albert Anastasia, who rose to infamy as the operating head of the underworld's enforcement arm, Murder, Inc.

descriptions: The Gagliano family (predecessor of Lucchese family) used a former Murder, Inc. hitman.
edges.evidence_quote: Working through Frankie Carbo , a former Murder, Inc. hitman, the Gagliano family managed a number of boxers...

descriptions: Murder, Inc. had close ties with the Italian-American Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The largely Jewish-American and Italian-American gang which was known as Murder, Inc. and Jewish mobsters such as Meyer Lansky ... developed close ties with the Italian-American Mafia

descriptions: Albert Anastasia, a leader of Murder, Inc., later became boss of the Gambino crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Albert Anastasia – Succeeded Buchalter until he became boss of the Gambino crime family

descriptions: In The Godfather, Murder Inc. is the armed wing of the Roth Syndicate.
edges.evidence_quote: Murder Inc. is revealed to be the execution armed wing of the Roth Syndicate

descriptions: Murder, Inc. carried out the Commission's order to kill Dutch Schultz.
edges.evidence_quote: the Commission ordered Louis Buchalter to eliminate 'The Dutchman'

descriptions: Murder, Inc. killed Willie Shapiro on orders from Buchalter, indicating a conflict between the Shapiro Brothers and Murder, Inc.
edges.evidence_quote: On orders from Buchalter, Willie was killed by Murder, Inc. members Martin "Bugsy" Goldstein and Abe "Kid Twist" Reles

descriptions: Alvaro and Filleti were on opposite sides in the Sinopoli feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Sinopoli: nasce nel 1945 con l'omicidio di Giuseppe Filleti tra gli Alvaro - Violi -Macrì e i Filleti-De Angelis-Orfeo

descriptions: Filleti and De Angelis were allied against Alvaro-Violi-Macrì in the Sinopoli feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Sinopoli: nasce nel 1945 con l'omicidio di Giuseppe Filleti tra gli Alvaro - Violi -Macrì e i Filleti-De Angelis-Orfeo

descriptions: Filleti and Orfeo were allied against Alvaro-Violi-Macrì in the Sinopoli feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Sinopoli: nasce nel 1945 con l'omicidio di Giuseppe Filleti tra gli Alvaro - Violi -Macrì e i Filleti-De Angelis-Orfeo

'Ndrina Zagari.description: The Zagari are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Taurianova, Italy, with operations in Northern Italy and Rome. They are allied with the Viola clan. In the late 1980s and early 1990s, they were involved in a violent feud with the Asciutto-Neri-Grimaldi clans, resulting in 32 murders. The clan's activities include extortion, illegal guardianship, and political infiltration.

descriptions: Zagari and Viola were in a violent feud with Asciutto-Neri-Grimaldi.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 2 luglio 1989 con l'uccisione di Rocco Neri scoppia una violenta faida tra gli Asciutto-Neri-Grimaldi e gli Zagari-Viola.

descriptions: Zagari and Avignone are part of the same criminal alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 12 dicembre 2017 si conclude l'operazione Terramara-Closed che porta all'arresto di 44 persone ... presunte affiliate ai Avignone -Zagari-Fazzalari-Viola

descriptions: Zagari and Fazzalari are part of the same criminal alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 12 dicembre 2017 si conclude l'operazione Terramara-Closed ... presunte affiliate ai Avignone -Zagari-Fazzalari-Viola

descriptions: Zagari is allied with Viola.
edges.evidence_quote: È alleata dei Viola , anch'essi di Taurianova .

descriptions: Sociedad Salvaje Silvestre is a cell of ITS.
edges.evidence_quote: In a statement the self-styled "Sociedad Salvaje Silvestre" cell took responsibility for the attack

descriptions: Russo clan (QS) listed as ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Russo clan (from the QS)

descriptions: West Covina is listed as a rival of Azusa 13 in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: West Covina

descriptions: Barriox13 has conflicts with CV Largo36.
edges.evidence_quote: Barriox13 remains active in its original territory and maintains conflicts with other Hispanic gangs, including CV155st, CV Largo36, CV117st, and others from Compton and South Central.

descriptions: Dojin-kai had a brief conflict with Sumiyoshi Union in 1983.
edges.evidence_quote: Another notable one is a brief conflict that occurred in 1983, with the Kanto-based large yakuza syndicate Sumiyoshi Union

Vagos Motorcycle Club.description: The Vagos Motorcycle Club is a one percenter outlaw motorcycle club formed in the 1960s in San Bernardino, California. With approximately 5,000 members across 200 chapters, it operates primarily in the Western United States, Mexico, and Europe. The club's insignia features Loki, the Norse god of mischief, riding a motorcycle. It is considered one of the largest and most powerful one-percenter biker gangs in the United States.

descriptions: Vagos MC listed as ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Dirty White Boys, Gambino crime family, Hells Angels, Mexican Mafia, Nazi Lowriders, Public Enemy No. 1, Sureños, Vagos MC

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood lists Vagos MC as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Vagos MC

descriptions: Infobox lists Vagos as rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Black Cobra, Comanchero MC, Cossacks MC, Finks MC, Gremium MC, Gypsy Joker MC, Kinfolk MC, Mongols MC, No Surrender MC, Notorious MC, Rebels MC, Red Devils MC, Rock Machine MC, Satudarah MC, Vagos MC

descriptions: Vagos are rivals of Bandidos.
edges.evidence_quote: the Hells Angels , the Mongols the Kinfolk , and the Vagos .

descriptions: Vagos are rivals of the Bandidos.
edges.evidence_quote: the Mongols the Kinfolk , and the Vagos

descriptions: Turf war between Bandidos and Vagos in New Mexico.
edges.evidence_quote: A turf war between the Bandidos and the Vagos emerged when the Vagos expanded into New Mexico from California.

descriptions: Vagos are listed as rivals of Bandidos.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos' rivals include the Cossacks, the Hells Angels, the Mongols the Kinfolk, and the Vagos.

descriptions: Dirty Dozen fought Vagos over expansion into Arizona in 1990.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1990, the Dirty Dozen became involved in a conflict with the Vagos after the California-based club expanded into Arizona, establishing a chapter in Phoenix.

descriptions: Vagos is a rival motorcycle gang of the Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: Rival motorcycle gangs include the ... Vagos

descriptions: Infobox lists Vagos MC as a rival from 2012 to 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Vagos MC, (2012–2015)

descriptions: The Loners were in conflict with the Vagos over territory in Peterborough.
edges.evidence_quote: The Loners were determined to compete the American-based club that exile Pammett was now aligning with.

descriptions: Loners and Vagos were in conflict, with attacks and arsons between them.
edges.evidence_quote: In July 2012, a 47-year-old member of the Loners Motorcycle Club was sent to hospital with a broken arm after being attacked by several former Loners, who were now affiliated with the Vagos.

descriptions: Loners Peterborough chapter had a conflict with Vagos Canada from 2012-2014.
edges.evidence_quote: When the Loners Peterborough chapter had a conflict with the Vagos Motorcycle Club Canada in 2012–2014, tensions between the two groups ended when the Vagos were absorbed by the returning Outlaws.

descriptions: Mongols consider Vagos as rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rival gangs include the Bandidos, the Hells Angels, the Iron Order, the Outlaws, and the Vagos.

descriptions: Nazi Lowriders have connections with Vagos.
edges.evidence_quote: The Nazi Lowiders also have connections with motorcycle gangs , including the Hells Angels , the Mongols , and the Vagos .

descriptions: Vagos and Galloping Goose were involved in a shootout on a highway.
edges.evidence_quote: On August 13, 2011, law enforcement authorities reported that the Vagos Motorcycle Club and the Galloping Goose Motorcycle Club were involved in a shootout which shut down traffic on I-44 near Waynesville, Missouri.

descriptions: Vagos and Hells Angels were involved in a violent shooting resulting in deaths and injuries.
edges.evidence_quote: On September 23, 2011, Vagos members were involved in a shooting at John Ascuaga's Nugget in Sparks, Nevada, where Jeffrey Pettigrew, the president of the San Jose, California chapter of Hells Angels was killed, and Vagos members were wounded.

descriptions: Vagos members in Ontario joined Outlaws Motorcycle Club by patching over in 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: Vagos in Ontario, Canada were reported to have patched over to Outlaws Motorcycle Club (American Outlaws Association) in 2015 before AOA disbanded in 2018.

descriptions: Winter Hill Gang fought and killed members of the Notarangeli gang.
edges.evidence_quote: the Winter Hill Gang subsequently began exterminating members of Notarangeli's gang

descriptions: Asa MC merged with released Brotherhood members to form the first outside chapter.
edges.evidence_quote: At the end of the year 1995 the outlaw motorcycle club (omc) Asa MC in Stockholm joined up with Brotherhood members that had been released from prison and founded the first Brotherhood chapter outside of the prison walls.

descriptions: Marriot brothers were allied with Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: Another gang allied to the Hells Angels were the Marriot brothers, Billy and Ricky, of Spryfield.

descriptions: Cohen worked as an enforcer for the Chicago Outfit.
edges.evidence_quote: During Prohibition, Cohen moved to Chicago and became involved in organized crime, working as an enforcer for the Chicago Outfit

descriptions: Cohen raised funds for Irgun.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1947 Ben Hecht of the Zionist American League for a Free Palestine approached Cohen asking for his help to raise funds for the Jewish paramilitary group Irgun. Cohen agreed and a fundraiser was held at his Slapsy Maxie's Café in LA, reportedly raising $200,000

descriptions: The Cohen crime family is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Cohen crime family

Catania Mafia family.description: The Catania Mafia family is a crime family of the Sicilian Cosa Nostra that dominates organized crime in Catania and its province. Founded around 1925 by Antonino Saitta, it evolved into a powerful and violent faction under leaders like Giuseppe Calderone and Benedetto Santapaola. The organization is involved in extortion, drug trafficking, and infiltration of legitimate businesses, and remains active today under the Santapaola-Ercolano clan.

descriptions: Catania Mafia family is listed as an ally of Pelle 'ndrina in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Barbaro 'ndrina , Catania Mafia family , Colombian drug cartels

descriptions: The Caltagirone Mafia allied with the Catania Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: His alliances with powerful Cosa Nostra factions, particularly the Catania Mafia and the Corleonesi led by Totò Riina, further elevated the status of the Caltagirone Mafia clan within the Sicilian Mafia hierarchy.

descriptions: The Caltagirone Mafia was loyal to the Santapaola Mafia clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Despite longstanding loyalty to the Santapaola Mafia clan, a brief moment of internal dissent occurred during Ciccio’s imprisonment in the late 1990s.

descriptions: The Caltagirone Mafia family is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Caltagirone Mafia family

descriptions: The Ercolano faction of the Catania Mafia family cooperated with Camorra groups.
edges.evidence_quote: often operating in tandem with powerful Camorra groups.

descriptions: The Santapaola clan and the Cappello-Cintorino clan were jointly running a criminal network involved in drug trafficking and extortion.
edges.evidence_quote: a large-scale anti-mafia operation ... exposed a vast criminal network run by the Cappello-Cintorino and Santapaola clans, deeply entrenched in drug trafficking, extortion, and territorial control

descriptions: The Cappello clan is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Cappello clan

descriptions: The Cappello clan is described as a rival clan to the Santapaola-Ercolano clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Russo also authorized extortion attempts on individuals like Aldo “u buttafuori,” a bouncer protected by a rival clan (the Cappello clan )

descriptions: The Casalesi clan is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Casalesi clan

descriptions: The Santapaola clan (Catania Mafia family) formed a criminal alliance with the Casalesi clan to fix prices in fruit and vegetable transport.
edges.evidence_quote: Italian authorities uncovered a criminal alliance between the Casalesi clan of the Camorra and the Santapaolas, forming what investigators describe as a price-fixing cartel

descriptions: The Catania Mafia family waged war against the Cursoti faction.
edges.evidence_quote: Santapaola launched a war against the Cursoti, a rival faction.

descriptions: Santapaola allied with the Corleonesi faction led by Totò Riina.
edges.evidence_quote: Nitto Santapaola began forming strategic ties with Totò Riina, a move Antonino Calderone would later call a 'Corleonese strategy.'

descriptions: A member of the Corso dei Mille family participated in the murder of Giuseppe Calderone alongside the Catania family's Santapaola faction.
edges.evidence_quote: Ignazio Ingrassia ... a man of honor from the Corso dei Mille family, closely associated with Michele Greco.

descriptions: The Mallardo clan is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Mallardo clan

descriptions: The Santapaola clan was arrested alongside members of the Mallardo clan in the same operation, indicating their involvement in the same criminal network.
edges.evidence_quote: around 60 individuals were arrested, including key members of the Camorra's Casalesi, Licciardi , and Mallardo clans , as well as the Santapaola clan

descriptions: The Mazzei clan attempted a coup against the Santapaola Mafia clan.
edges.evidence_quote: His nephew, Gesualdo La Rocca, supported an attempted coup by the rival Mazzei clan to displace the Santapaola leadership in Catania.

descriptions: The Passo di Rigano Mafia family is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Passo di Rigano Mafia family

descriptions: The Pietraperzia Mafia family is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Pietraperzia Mafia family

descriptions: The Ramacca Mafia family is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Ramacca Mafia family

descriptions: The Riesi Mafia family is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Riesi Mafia family

descriptions: The Santa Maria di Gesù Mafia family is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Santa Maria di Gesù Mafia family

descriptions: The Vallelunga Pratameno family participated in the murder of Giuseppe Calderone alongside the Catania family's Santapaola faction.
edges.evidence_quote: collaboration among multiple families from different provinces, all linked to the Corleonesi, underscores the coordinated effort behind Calderone’s elimination.

descriptions: Clan Santapaola had strong resentment towards Clan Cappello.
edges.evidence_quote: Nitto Santapaola nutriva un forte rancore nei confronti di Turi Cappello

descriptions: The Barcellonesi faction was allied with the Santapaola clan of Catania.
edges.evidence_quote: l'ala rimasta "barcellonese" era alleata della fazione dei Santapaola di Cosa Nostra catanese

descriptions: Casalesi clan cooperated with Catania Mafia family.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2019, the Direzione Investigativa Antimafia of Catania dismantled a scheme between the Camorra's Mallardo clan and Casalesi clan and members of the powerful Catania Mafia family , led by Vincenzo Enrico Augusto Ercolano, son of Giuseppe Ercolano (deceased, but once an important member of the Catania's mafia) and Grazia Santapaola, the sister of the historical boss Benedetto Santapaola .

descriptions: The Catania Mafia family is listed as an ally of the Casalesi clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Catania Mafia family

descriptions: The Casalesi clan allied with the Santapaola clan in a price-fixing cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Italian authorities have uncovered a criminal alliance between the Casalesi clan and the Catania Mafia family, headed by the Santapaola-Ercolano faction

descriptions: The Santapaola clan was allied with the Laudani clan and thus supported the Catania faction of Cursoti.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 1991 scoppiò una violenta faida, che vide contrapposti da un lato i Cursoti catanesi, guidati da Giuseppe Garozzo e da Santo Mazzei e appoggiati dal clan Laudani (e quindi dai Santapaola , Ferrera, Ercolano e dai Malpassoti ), e dall’altro i milanesi, facenti capo a Jimmy Miano e supportati dal clan Cappello

descriptions: Clan Ferlito-Pillera and Clan Santapaola were in a bloody conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Lo scontro tra i clan Ferlito-Pillera e Santapaola fu il più cruento della storia della mafia catanese

descriptions: Ciro Mazzarella had strong relations with the Catania Mafia family.
edges.evidence_quote: Ciro Mazzarella ... having strong relations with the Sicilian Mafia, in particular with the Catania Mafia family.

descriptions: The Catania family was part of the Corleonesi coalition.
edges.evidence_quote: Nitto Santapaola from the Catania Mafia family

descriptions: The Inzerillo clan lists the Catania Mafia family as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Catania Mafia family

descriptions: Mallardo clan cooperated with Catania Mafia family.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2019, the Direzione Investigativa Antimafia of Catania dismantled a scheme between the Camorra's Mallardo clan and Casalesi clan and members of the powerful Catania Mafia family , led by Vincenzo Enrico Augusto Ercolano, son of Giuseppe Ercolano (deceased, but once an important member of the Catania's mafia) and Grazia Santapaola, the sister of the historical boss Benedetto Santapaola .

descriptions: Catania Mafia family is an ally of the Mallardo clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Catania Mafia family

descriptions: Mallardo clan allied with Catania Mafia family to control wholesale markets.
edges.evidence_quote: The Mallardo and Casalesi clans in an alliance with the Catania Mafia family of the Cosa Nostra, had established violent control over key wholesale markets in Fondi and Giugliano.

descriptions: Minore clan had close ties with Catania Mafia family.
edges.evidence_quote: Totò Minore developed very close ties to the Catania Mafia family

descriptions: Pietraperzia is a close ally of the Catania Mafia family.
edges.evidence_quote: Currently, the Pietraperzia family is regarded as a close ally of the powerful Catania Mafia family.

descriptions: Ramacca Mafia family is allied with Catania Mafia family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Catania Mafia family , Caltagirone Mafia family , Corleone Mafia family

Philadelphia Greek Mob.description: The Philadelphia Greek Mob was a loose-knit Greek-American criminal faction operating in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, closely allied with the Italian-American Philadelphia crime family. Active primarily from the 1970s to the 1980s, it engaged in racketeering, drug trafficking, extortion, loansharking, and illegal gambling. The group was led by Chelsais "Steve" Bouras until his assassination in 1981, after which its influence declined.

descriptions: The K&A Gang had amicable ties with the local Greek mob.
edges.evidence_quote: the local Greek mob

descriptions: Scarfo had Bouras killed for refusing to pay street tax, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: When Greek-American gangster Chelsais "Steve" Bouras, boss of the Philadelphia Greek Mob, began intruding on the methamphetamine trade in Philadelphia and refused to pay Scarfo's street tax, Scarfo had him killed.

descriptions: Listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Philadelphia Greek Mob

descriptions: Greek mob was a close ally and partner of Philadelphia Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Although the Greek mob had long been a close ally and partner of the Philadelphia Mafia...

descriptions: The infobox lists the K&A Gang as an ally of the Philadelphia Greek Mob.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: K&A Gang , Philadelphia crime family

descriptions: The Philadelphia Greek Mob was closely affiliated with and later fell under complete control of the Philadelphia crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The group was closely affiliated with the Italian-American Philadelphia crime family, and fell under complete control of the family during the reign of Philadelphia Mafia boss Nicky Scarfo.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Philadelphia Greek Mob as an ally of the Velentzas Organization.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lucchese crime family, Philadelphia Greek Mob

Popular Liberation Army.description: Colombian left-wing guerrilla organization founded in 1967, based on Marxism-Leninism. Most of its members demobilized in 1991, but a dissident faction remains active in the Catatumbo region and the Colombia-Venezuela border. It is classified as an Organized Armed Group by the Colombian government.

descriptions: AUC was formed to combat disidencias of EPL.
edges.evidence_quote: Se consolidó como agrupación de varios grupos paramilitares el 18 de abril de 1997 para combatir a las guerrillas como: FARC-EP , ELN y disidencias del EPL .

descriptions: The AUC was formed to combat dissidents of the EPL guerrilla group.
edges.evidence_quote: Se consolidó como agrupación de varios grupos paramilitares el 18 de abril de 1997 para combatir a las guerrillas como: FARC-EP , ELN y disidencias del EPL .

descriptions: The AUC was formed to combat dissidences of the EPL guerrilla group.
edges.evidence_quote: Se consolidó como agrupación de varios grupos paramilitares el 18 de abril de 1997 para combatir a las guerrillas como: FARC-EP , ELN y disidencias del EPL .

descriptions: People's Liberation Army is the armed wing of the Communist Party of Burma.
edges.evidence_quote: The Communist Party of Burma rearmed itself and announced the creation of its new armed wing, the People's Liberation Army, in late 2021.

descriptions: Popular Liberation Army is listed as an ally of CJNG in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Milenio Cartel , Juárez Cartel , La Línea (gang) , Caborca Cartel , Gulf Cartel , Primeiro Comando da Capital , MS-13 , Clan del Golfo , Popular Liberation Army , FARC-EP , 'Ndrangheta , Guerreros Unidos , Camorra , Nuestra Familia

edges.source: Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia
descriptions: Puntilla (leader of ERPAC's successor) had a business relationship with the EPL dissident group.
edges.evidence_quote: con el cual mantenía una relación de negocios

descriptions: Los Rastrojos are in territorial war with EPL dissidents.
edges.evidence_quote: guerra territorial que hay entre lo que queda de la disidencia del EPL y los llamados Rastrojos contra la guerrilla del ELN

descriptions: Los Rastrojos are allied with Los Pelusos.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Los Pelusos

edges.source: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: Demobilized EPL members integrated into ACCU.
edges.evidence_quote: la integración de desmovilizados del EPL en las ACCU

descriptions: EPL and M-19 were allies until 1990.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: M-19 ( Hasta 1990 )

descriptions: EPL considered AUC as enemies until 2006.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: AUC ( Hasta 2006 )

descriptions: Pablo Escobar of the Cartel de Medellín sought help from EPL but no agreement was reached.
edges.evidence_quote: el capo buscó la ayuda del EPL para refugiarse con ellos junto a su escolta Álvaro Agudelo, El Limón , ya que el grupo de Los Pepes , financiado por el Cartel de Cali y los paramilitares , buscaban asesinar al jefe del Cartel de Medellín ; al final, la guerrilla y el narcotraficante no llegaron a un acuerdo de protección

descriptions: Infobox lists Clan del Golfo as an opponent of the EPL.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Clan del Golfo

descriptions: Infobox lists Group of Popular Combatants as an ally of the EPL.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Group of Popular Combatants , PCP (m-l) , FARC

descriptions: Remnants of Los Rastrojos allied with EPL to form a new criminal structure.
edges.evidence_quote: un reducto de Los Rastrojos en el Catatumbo se habría aliado con el EPL para conformar una nueva estructura criminal

descriptions: EPL allied with Los Rastrojos to fight ELN.
edges.evidence_quote: quienes presuntamente habrían hecho una alianza con lo que quedó del grupo narcoparamilitar Los Rastrojos para combatir al ELN

descriptions: EPL is in confrontation with ELN over territorial control and declared war on ELN.
edges.evidence_quote: En esta zona, mantiene una confrontación con el Ejército de Liberación Nacional (ELN) por el control territorial; en su momento, la guerrilla le declaró oficialmente la guerra al ELN «hasta recuperar lo que ellos consideran su territorio y su gente».

descriptions: EPL considered ELN as enemies until 2021.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ELN ( hasta 2021 )

descriptions: EPL declared war on ELN in 2018, ending a tacit alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: el EPL le declara la guerra al Frente de Guerra Nororiental del Ejército de Liberación Nacional (ELN) desde el 20 de marzo de 2018, terminando de esta manera la alianza tácita que tenían estos dos grupos

descriptions: Los Pelusos are at war with the ELN in the Catatumbo region.
edges.evidence_quote: afectados por la guerra que sostienen con el ELN en la región

edges.target: Partido Comunista de Colombia - Marxista Leninista
descriptions: EPL was the armed wing of the Partido Comunista de Colombia - Marxista Leninista.
edges.evidence_quote: se constituyó como el brazo armado del Partido Comunista de Colombia - Marxista Leninista

edges.target: Partido Comunista de Colombia - Marxista Leninista
descriptions: EPL was the military wing of PCdeC-ML.
edges.evidence_quote: El EPL era el brazo militar del Partido Comunista de Colombia - Marxista Leninista

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Group of Popular Combatants , PCP (m-l) , FARC

descriptions: The Pedro León Arboleda Movement is a splinter group from the EPL.
edges.evidence_quote: A small splinter group, the Pedro León Arboleda Movement , named after a deceased 1975 commander, had been created in 1979.

descriptions: EPL and FARC-EP were allies until 2016.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: FARC-EP ( Hasta 2016 )

descriptions: EPL conducted joint actions with FARC-EP.
edges.evidence_quote: Realizó algunas acciones en conjunto con la guerrilla de las FARC-EP, como la realizada en Hacarí (Norte de Santander).

descriptions: Infobox lists FARC as an ally of the EPL.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Group of Popular Combatants , PCP (m-l) , FARC

descriptions: EPL leader Megateo established contacts with Sinaloa Cartel for cocaine export.
edges.evidence_quote: Megateo inició los contactos internacionales de esta guerrilla con el Cartel de Sinaloa para exportar cocaína

descriptions: EPL is the name of the paramilitary wing under the Mantaro faction since 2001.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 2001, it has been operated by the Mantaro faction under the name of People's Liberation Army ( Ejército Popular de Liberación , EPL ).

Kosovo Liberation Army.description: Ethnic Albanian separatist militia that sought Kosovo's secession from Yugoslavia during the 1990s. Founded in 1994, it engaged in armed conflict with Yugoslav forces, leading to NATO intervention in 1999. Officially disbanded in September 1999, with members transitioning to the Kosovo Protection Corps. Former leaders later entered politics, and some faced war crimes indictments.

edges.target: Liberation Army of Preševo, Medveđa and Bujanovac
descriptions: UÇPMB was formed after the Kosovo War, with former KLA fighters among its members.
edges.evidence_quote: In June 1999, a new Albanian militant insurgent group was formed under the Liberation Army of Preševo, Medveđa and Bujanovac (UÇPMB), which started training in the GSZ.

descriptions: LPK was a precursor that played a crucial role in creating the KLA.
edges.evidence_quote: A key precursor to the Kosovo Liberation Army was the People's Movement of Kosovo (LPK).

descriptions: The Serb mafia is a rival of UÇK.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Princ Gang , UÇK , Naser Gang

Los Talibanes.description: Mexican criminal organization based in San Luis Potosí and Zacatecas, founded in 2012 by Iván Velázquez Caballero, a former member of Los Zetas. The group broke from Los Zetas and allied with the Gulf Cartel. Engaged in drug trafficking, particularly fentanyl, and migrant smuggling. Since 2019, they have fought alongside the Jalisco New Generation Cartel and Sinaloa Cartel for control of Zacatecas.

descriptions: Los Talibanes faced Jalisco New Generation Cartel in clashes.
edges.evidence_quote: being allied with the Sinaloa Cartel, and facing the Jalisco New Generation Cartel in bloody clashes

descriptions: Infobox lists Jalisco New Generation Cartel as rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mexico , Jalisco New Generation Cartel , Los Zetas

descriptions: Los Talibanes are fighting against CJNG.
edges.evidence_quote: enfrentando al Cártel Jalisco Nueva Generación en cruentos enfrentamientos

descriptions: Los Talibanes fought alongside Jalisco New Generation Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Since the end of 2019, Los Talibanes have been fighting alongside the Jalisco New Generation Cartel and the Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: Los Talibanes allied with Gulf Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: allied himself with the Gulf Cartel to displace his former partner.

descriptions: Infobox lists Gulf Cartel as ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gulf Cartel

descriptions: Los Talibanes allied with the Gulf Cartel to fight against Los Zetas.
edges.evidence_quote: se alió con el Cártel del Golfo para desplazar a su antiguo compañero.

descriptions: Los Talibanes fought alongside Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Since the end of 2019, Los Talibanes have been fighting alongside the Jalisco New Generation Cartel and the Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: Los Talibanes are allied with the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: estando aliado al Cártel de Sinaloa

American Front.description: White supremacist organization founded in San Francisco, California by Bob Heick in 1984. Originally modeled after the British National Front, it later transformed into a political organization with skinhead and non-skinhead members. The group held White Workers Day marches and published the tabloid Aryan Warrior. By 1989, it had units in 14 American states.

descriptions: American Front's magazine had ties to Combat 18.
edges.evidence_quote: the Washington and California AF sections published The Voice of Revolution magazine, which had ties to Combat 18 in England.

descriptions: Tension and disagreement arose between American Front and White Aryan Resistance after Aryan Woodstock.
edges.evidence_quote: Tension between AF and WAR increased soon after.

descriptions: American Front cooperated with White Aryan Resistance starting in 1988.
edges.evidence_quote: Heick began working with Tom Metzger 's White Aryan Resistance (WAR) in 1988.

El Forastero Motorcycle Club.description: One-percenter motorcycle club founded in 1962 by Tom Fugle and Harlan 'Tiny' Brower in Sioux City, Iowa. Known for criminal activities including motorcycle theft and methamphetamine distribution. Considered a second-tier outlaw club after the 'Big Four'. Has chapters in Iowa, Minnesota, Kansas, and Missouri.

descriptions: El Forastero has close links to Galloping Goose MC, and members of both clubs pooled money to buy drugs together.
edges.evidence_quote: The club was founded in 1962 by Tom Fugle and Harlan "Tiny" Brower has chapters in Iowa, Minnesota, Kansas, and Missouri and close links to the Galloping Goose MC .

descriptions: Satan Slaves MC rejected El Forastero's application for a chapter, leading to El Forastero's independent founding.
edges.evidence_quote: El Forastero Motorcycle Club ( EFMC ) is a one-percenter motorcycle club which was established after being turned down for a chapter by the Satan Slaves MC.

descriptions: The text states a close relationship between Galloping Goose and El Forastero.
edges.evidence_quote: The club has a close relationship with El Forastero Motorcycle Club.

descriptions: El Forastero is a smaller motorcycle gang allied with Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels are allied with numerous smaller motorcycle gangs – such as the ... El Forastero

descriptions: Infobox lists El Forastero MC as rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Devils Diciples MC, Diablos MC, El Forastero MC, Galloping Goose MC, Hells Angels MC, Highwaymen MC, Iron Horsemen MC, Loners MC, Mongols MC, Pagans MC, Red Devils MC, Sons of Silence MC, Warlocks MC

descriptions: El Forastero is a rival biker gang of the Outlaws.
edges.evidence_quote: Rival biker gangs of the Outlaws include the Devils Diciples, the Diablos, El Forastero

Wo Shing Wo.description: Wo Shing Wo is the oldest of the Wo Group triad societies in Hong Kong, founded in Sham Shui Po in 1930. It is involved in extortion, drug trafficking, gambling, and prostitution. The group has expanded its influence from Tsuen Wan to other parts of Hong Kong and operates globally, including in China, Macau, and Europe. It continues to use traditional Hongmen initiation ceremonies.

descriptions: 14K and Wo Shing Wo are rivals in Ireland and UK, involved in turf wars.
edges.evidence_quote: The reported criminal activities of the triads included the trafficking of women and children from China into Ireland, involvement in casinos, and money laundering. Gardaí also reported a great deal of interaction between the Chinese gangs operating in Ireland and Scotland.

descriptions: Ah Kong members had a dispute with Wo Shing Wo, resulting in violence.
edges.evidence_quote: two members of Ah Kong... went to settle a dispute with the Wo Shing Wo triad in Rotterdam

descriptions: Big Circle Gang lists Wo Shing Wo as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Company, American Mafia, Red Wa, Fujian gangs, Wo Shing Wo, Menace of Destruction, Asian Boyz, MS-13, Netas

descriptions: Wo Shing Wo is a constituent triad of Sam Gor.
edges.evidence_quote: Sam Gor is made up of 14K, Wo Shing Wo, Sun Yee On, Big Circle Gang and Bamboo Union.

descriptions: The infobox lists Wo Shing Wo as a rival of Shui Fong.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: 14K, Wo Shing Wo

descriptions: A Shui Fong associate was murdered after refusing to do business with Wo Shing Wo, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: It is believed Wong was murdered after refusing to do business with the Wo Shing Wo who wanted a share of his lucrative Chinese video rental business.

descriptions: Wo Shing Wo members murdered a Sun Yee On boss, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Lee Tai-lung (李泰龍), a Sun Yee On boss in Tsim Sha Tsui, was murdered in front of the Kowloon Shangri-La hotel on 4 August 2009 by members of the Wo Shing Wo gang.

descriptions: Wo Shing Wo and 14K are rivals in Ireland.
edges.evidence_quote: the activities of the Wo Shing Wo and their rival 14K

descriptions: Wo Shing Wo battled 14K for heroin trade control in Amsterdam in the 1970s.
edges.evidence_quote: In the 1970s, the group battled the 14K and Ah Kong for control of the heroin trade in Amsterdam.

descriptions: Wo Shing Wo attacked 14K members in Glasgow in 2003.
edges.evidence_quote: Wo Shing Wo footsoldiers ambushed 14K members in a machete attack on Glasgow's Sauchiehall Street in July 2003

descriptions: Wo Shing Wo battled Ah Kong for heroin trade control in Amsterdam in the 1970s.
edges.evidence_quote: In the 1970s, the group battled the 14K and Ah Kong for control of the heroin trade in Amsterdam.

descriptions: Big Circle Gang is listed as an ally of Wo Shing Wo in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Big Circle Gang

descriptions: Wo On Lok members attacked Wo Shing Wo members.
edges.evidence_quote: Wo Shing Wo members are attacked by members of Wo on Lok.

descriptions: A Wo Shing Wo leader masterminded the murder of a Sun Yee On leader.
edges.evidence_quote: Leung Kwok-chung, aka "Tattooed Chung" (high-ranking leader of the Tai Kok Tsui branch of Wo Shing Wo and the mastermind behind the murder of Lee Tai-lung, the dragon head of Tsim Sha Tsui branch of Sun Yee On ).

descriptions: Sun Yee On is listed as a rival of Wo Shing Wo in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: 14K , Ah Kong , Sun Yee On , Wo On Lok

descriptions: Wo Shing Wo in Toronto was established as a branch of Wo Hop To.
edges.evidence_quote: The Wo Shing Wo was established in Toronto in 1930 as an international branch of the Wo Hop To

descriptions: Las Águilas is a subgroup of Los Choneros.
edges.evidence_quote: Esta organización criminal y delincuencial ... está conformada por dos peligrosas ramificaciones delictivas llamadas ... ' Las Águilas'

descriptions: Filthy Rebels were a Hells Angels-affiliated gang that later became a Hells Angels chapter.
edges.evidence_quote: The Filthy Rebels, a Hells Angels-affiliated gang in Grenoble... were subsequently 'patched over' by the Hells Angels on 7 September 1991 to become the club's Grenoble chapter.

Texas Syndicate.description: The Texas Syndicate is a mostly Texas-based street and prison gang with predominantly Mexican American membership, founded in 1978 at Folsom State Prison in California. It was established for self-protection against other prison gangs and later engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, prostitution, gambling, and contract murder. The gang has approximately 1,300 members and maintains a paramilitary structure. It operates primarily in Texas but also in prisons across the United States.

descriptions: ABT fought wars with Texas Syndicate in 1995-1996, resulting in TM being 'stationed'.
edges.evidence_quote: La Mafia Texana (TM) estuvo «estacionada» en todo el sistema, lo que significa que ya no se les permitía reclutar miembros, después de las guerras ABT-TM en 1995 a 1996.

descriptions: Texas Syndicate is listed as a rival of BGF in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Aryan Brotherhood , Aryan Brotherhood of Texas , Mexican Mafia , Texas Syndicate

descriptions: Gulf Cartel employs Texas Syndicate for drug distribution.
edges.evidence_quote: The Mexican drug cartels that operate in the area are currently employing gang members to distribute drugs and conduct other criminal activities on their behalf. Among these gangs... are the Texas Syndicate

descriptions: Infobox lists Sindicato de Texas as an ally of CDG.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Sindicato de Texas

descriptions: Los Zetas and Texas Syndicate are allies per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Tijuana Cartel , Juárez Cartel , 'Ndrangheta , Los Mazatlecos , Barrio Azteca , MS-13 , Texas Syndicate , Triad , The Office of Envigado , La Línea , Hezbollah , Sicilian Mafia , Los Zetas Group Bravo

descriptions: Mexikanemi has a rivalry with the Texas Syndicate.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Nuestra Familia , Texas Syndicate

descriptions: Puro Tango Blast formed for protection from Texas Syndicate, indicating a conflict relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Incarcerated Hispanic men from major Texas cities (including Houston , Dallas , Fort Worth , Austin ), have banded together for protection from established security threat groups like Mexikanemi and the Texas Syndicate .

descriptions: Texas Syndicate was formed in response to Aryan Brotherhood preying on Texas inmates, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The Texas Syndicate was established at Folsom State Prison in California in 1978 in direct response to the other California prison gangs, notably the Aryan Brotherhood and Mexican Mafia, which were attempting to prey on native Texas inmates.

descriptions: El Sindicato de Texas se formó como respuesta a la Hermandad Aria, que se aprovechaba de reclusos texanos.
edges.evidence_quote: como respuesta a otras pandillas que surgieron en la década, sobre todo a la Hermandad Aria y la Mafia Mexicana, que intentaban aprovecharse de los reclusos nativos de Texas.

descriptions: La infobox lista a la Hermandad Aria como enemigo del Sindicato de Texas.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Hermandad Aria, Mafia mexicana, Mexikanemi, Nuestra Familia, Puro Tango Blast

edges.evidence_quote: Los miembros de la pandilla tienen una relación de trabajo directa con asociados y/o miembros del Cártel del Golfo.

descriptions: Texas Syndicate members perform contract killings for Los Zetas, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Syndicate members have been known to carry out contract killings for Los Zetas.

descriptions: Texas Syndicate was formed in response to Mexican Mafia preying on Texas inmates, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The Texas Syndicate was established at Folsom State Prison in California in 1978 in direct response to the other California prison gangs, notably the Aryan Brotherhood and Mexican Mafia, which were attempting to prey on native Texas inmates.

edges.evidence_quote: El Sindicato de Texas, a diferencia de la Mafia Mexicana o Nuestra Familia, ha estado más asociado o aliado con prisioneros inmigrantes mexicanos.

descriptions: May 19th Communist Organization allied with Black Liberation Army to commit the Brink's robbery.
edges.evidence_quote: three former members, Kathy Boudin, Judith Alice Clark, and David Gilbert, joined the May 19 Communist Organization, and on October 20, 1981, in Nanuet, New York, the group helped the Black Liberation Army rob a Brink's armored truck

descriptions: Former WUO members joined the May 19th Communist Organization after WUO dissolved.
edges.evidence_quote: Some members of the group joined the May 19th Communist Organization and continued their activities until that group disbanded in 1985.

descriptions: May 19th Communist Organization was a splinter faction of the Weather Underground.
edges.evidence_quote: by the late 1970s, the Weatherman group had further split into two factions—the May 19th Communist Organization and the Prairie Fire Collective
